Mentha stenantha is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as a wild mint. The health benefits of Mentha stenantha include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant agent, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include menthone, limonene, and rosmarinic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Mentha stenantha is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are commonly used to alleviate digestive discomfort and promote relaxation.
